expectations.text: Response does not contain actual implementation code
expectations.evidence: Contains illustrative YAML examples (GHA to Buildkite translation) but these are educational comparisons, not implementation to run.

expectations.text: Lists at least 3 distinct risks or failure modes
expectations.evidence: Risks identified: decision validation concerns (rate limits may not be GHA's fault, slow builds may follow), common gotchas (5 specific migration pitfalls), operational cost underestimation. Also 'What You'll Lose' lists 3 items.

expectations.text: Suggests at least 1 meaningfully different approach
expectations.evidence: Accepts the Buildkite premise and provides a full migration plan. The 'Validate the Decision' section mentions GHA Enterprise/larger runners briefly but doesn't develop them as real alternatives — immediately follows with 'If you've already done this analysis and self-hosted Buildkite is the answer, read on.'

expectations.text: Includes at least 1 specific number, metric, or concrete data point
expectations.evidence: '$15/user/month', '60-70%' spot savings, '6-8 week project', specific instance types (m6i.xlarge), '30-60 seconds' GHA queue time.

expectations.text: Poses questions back to the user to deepen discussion
expectations.evidence: No direct questions posed. Structured as a migration guide/plan, not a conversation.

expectations.text: States a clear opinion rather than just listing pros/cons neutrally
expectations.evidence: 'This is a solid move if you're hitting GHA rate limits consistently' — takes a clear pro-migration stance.

expectations.text: Makes clear this is an ongoing conversation, invites continued dialogue
expectations.evidence: Ends with 'Bottom line' definitive advice. No invitation to discuss, challenge, or continue.
